subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Cost of Storing Everything
description: A fast breakdown of The Cost of Storing Everything for builders.

hook.text: A company stores 10 petabytes of data 'just in case.' Annual cost: $250,000. Nobody has queried most of it in years.

buildup.text: Cloud storage is cheap per gigabyte. But at scale, keeping everything adds up to significant ongoing expense.

discovery.text: Data retention policies define what to keep, for how long, and when to delete. Compliance often dictates minimums.

twist.text: Deleting data is emotionally hard. Teams hoard data because 'we might need it someday.' Someday rarely comes.

climax.text: The discipline of data lifecycle management separates cost-efficient teams from those drowning in unused storage.

punchline.text: Just because you can store everything doesn't mean you should.

quiz.question: Why do many teams struggle with cloud storage costs?
options: They hoard data indefinitely instead of implementing retention policies
Cloud storage prices increase every year by 50%
Data cannot be deleted once stored in the cloud
